title: [Question]How to have mRemoteNG use KiTTY instead of PuTTY ?
author: sebma
body_snippet:
How to have mRemoteNG use KiTTY instead of PuTTY ?

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version used: 1.76.20.24615
* Operating System and version : Windows 10 Pro v1903

iterations.description: The user is requesting support for KiTTY, a fork of PuTTY, which would enhance mRemoteNG's functionality by providing an alternative terminal client. This is a new feature.

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: we are thinking about such, but at the moment its not implemented

comments.author: thetuxinator
comments.snippet: Not sure what the intention is to add kitty support, as i can't see a usecase for it, as mremote already brings "tab support" "scp support" and more.

comments.author: sebma

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The user is requesting support for KiTTY, a fork of PuTTY, which would enhance mRemoteNG's functionality by providing an alternative terminal client. This is a new feature.
Approach: Investigate KiTTY's command-line arguments and registry structure for session management. Implement a dedicated KiTTY session manager or extend the existing PuTTY session manager to support KiTTY. Create a new 'KittyBase' class (potentially inheriting from a refactored 'TerminalBase' or 'PuttyBase') to handle KiTTY-specific protocol interactions. Update UI components to allow selection and configuration of KiTTY, including a custom path setting in the Advanced Options. Ensure appropriate detection logic for KiTTY versions and provide new unit tests for KiTTY integration.
